data.description:
# Pin the release supply chain to immutable references

Make every executable the release depends on identifiable by an immutable
reference: full-SHA action pins, a hash-locked build environment, an
exact-versioned + digest-verified MCP publisher, and non-persisted checkout
credentials. (Readiness Finding 4 — verified VALID against `release.yml`
lines 31/32/43/60/65/85/124/129 and `pyproject.toml:1-3`.)

## Why

Two release runs of the same rebar SHA can execute different build/upload/publish
code today. `release.yml` references first-party actions by moving major-version
tags (`actions/checkout@v7` at :31/:124, `actions/setup-python@v6` at :32,
`actions/upload-artifact@v7` at :43, `actions/download-artifact@v8` at :60/:85);
installs build tools with unrestricted upgrades (`pip install --upgrade build`
:37, `--upgrade twine` :41); publishes via the branch-like `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1`
(:65); declares the build backend as bare `hatchling` (`pyproject.toml:1-3`); and
in the `mcp_registry` job downloads its publisher from a mutable `releases/latest`
URL and executes it **without a digest/signature check** (:129) in a job that holds
`id-token: write` (used for GitHub OIDC login + registry publication). None of the
checkouts set `persist-credentials: false`, so the token persists in local git
config by default. The repo already SHA-pins actions in `gerrit-verify.yaml` and
`_optionality.yml`, so `release.yml` is inconsistent with the repo's own convention.

## What

Adopt the uv / pip pinning pattern (uv: full-SHA action pins, `persist-credentials:
false`, a pinned release tool with a verified SHA-256; pip: SHA-pinned actions +
a hash-locked build environment installed with `--require-hashes --no-deps`):

- Replace each action tag with a full commit SHA, keeping the human-readable
  version in a trailing comment.
- Set `persist-credentials: false` on every release checkout unless a later git
  operation demonstrably needs the persisted credential.
- Create a reviewed release lock/constraints file covering `build`, Hatchling,
  Twine, any artifact-validation tools, and their transitive deps; install it with
  hashes and build without further isolated resolution.
- Replace the MCP publisher `releases/latest` URL with an exact version, store the
  expected checksum/signature in the repo, and verify it before extraction/execution;
  fail on an unexpected filename/size/digest/version.
- Print and retain in release evidence: every action SHA, the Python build-tool
  versions, the MCP publisher version + downloaded digest, and the wheel/sdist digests.
- Update pins through reviewed dependency-update changes, not implicit resolution
  during a production release.

## Scope

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — SHA-pin all `uses:`; `persist-credentials:
  false`; hash-locked build install; exact-version + digest-verified MCP publisher
  (coordinate with Findings 1 and 3, which also edit this file).
- A new release build lock/constraints file (e.g. under `.github/` or `requirements/`).
- `pyproject.toml` — no functional change required to the unpinned `[build-system]`
  requires (the lock governs the isolated build env), but document the intent.
- `docs/releasing.md` — document the pinning policy and the pin-update process.
- Out of scope: pinning non-release workflows already SHA-pinned; full reproducible
  builds (covered adjacent to Finding 3).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every `uses:` in `release.yml` references a full 40-char commit SHA with a
  trailing version comment (demonstrated by Experiment 4A: a moved tag no longer
  changes the executed action code).
- [ ] Every release checkout sets `persist-credentials: false`.
- [ ] Release build tools (`build`, Hatchling, Twine, validation tools) install from
  a reviewed hash-locked file with `--require-hashes` (demonstrated by Experiment
  4C: repeated locked builds resolve identical tool versions).
- [ ] The MCP publisher is pinned to an exact version and its archive digest is
  verified before execution, failing on a mismatch (demonstrated by Experiment 4B:
  a replaced binary is rejected before execution).
- [ ] Release evidence records all action SHAs, build-tool versions, the MCP
  publisher version + digest, and the wheel/sdist digests.
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md` documents the pinning policy and pin-update process.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass, and the change lands via the
  Gerrit two-vote gate.
data.title: Pin the release supply chain to immutable references
